intro: Stenodraba fastigiata is a plant species native to Australia, belonging to the family Brassicaceae, commonly known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Stenodraba fastigiata include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects.
